nodes.name: Schedule — Sunday 22:00 UTC (Mon 8AM AEST)
nodes.notes: Fires Sunday 22:00 UTC = Monday 08:00 AEST. Delivers weekly Workspace update brief.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ model: $env.QWEN_MODEL || 'qwen3:14b', messages: [{ role: 'system', content: $json.system }, { role: 'user', content: $json.user }], stream: false, options: { temperature: 0.3 } }) }}

nodes.name: POST — Ollama Qwen 3 14B
nodes.notes: Calls local Ollama. Requires OLLAMA_BASE_URL env var (default: http://localhost:11434) and qwen3:14b model pulled via 'ollama pull qwen3:14b'.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ chat_id: $env.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, text: '📋 *Weekly Workspace Intel*\n\nNo keyword-matching updates captured this week. RSS feed polled — no Gemini, MCP, NotebookLM or agent-related posts found.', parse_mode: 'Markdown' }) }}

meta.description: RA-826 — Every Monday 8AM AEST, fetches the last 7 days of stored Google Workspace intel from Pi-CEO, generates an executive brief via Qwen 3 14B (Ollama), and delivers it to Telegram.

environment_variables_required: PI_CEO_WEBHOOK_SECRET  — value of TAO_WEBHOOK_SECRET from Railway env vars
OLLAMA_BASE_URL        — default: http://localhost:11434 (Ollama must be running locally with n8n)
QWEN_MODEL             — default: qwen3:14b (run 'ollama pull qwen3:14b' to download)
prerequisites: Qwen model downloaded: ollama pull qwen3:14b
RA-826 monitor workflow (workspace-intel-monitor.json) must be active to populate intel
_setup.import_instructions: In n8n: Workflows → Import from file → select this JSON. Configure env vars in n8n Settings → Environment Variables. Activate the workflow. Test manually via 'Execute Workflow' button.
